hawkish_phrases: there are limits to the extent that above-target inflation can be tolerated
inflation is expected to increase to 2.8% in the first half of 2018
if, on the other hand, pay growth picks up by more than anticipated, monetary policy may need to be tightened to a greater degree
further substantial increases are very likely over the coming months
the continuing suitability of the current policy stance depends on the trade-off between above-target inflation and slack in the economy
dovish_phrases: if spending growth slows more abruptly than expected, there is scope for monetary policy to be loosened
Attempting to offset fully the effect of weaker sterling on inflation would be achievable only at the cost of higher unemployment
it remained appropriate to seek to return inflation to the target over a somewhat longer period than usual
the current stance of monetary policy remained appropriate
Monetary policy can respond, in either direction
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.25% with balanced two-way guidance, tolerating above-target inflation while noting limits to such tolerance and conditional tightening bias if pay growth picks up more than expected.
